title: Maharashtra records highest number of 24,171 cases in crimes against children: NCRB report
content: The cases recorded concerning sexual offences against minors, economic and cyber offences, reflect that the crime in Maharashtra has increased

title: ‘Value‑added cashew apple products yet to reach people’
content: ‘Although two government research institutes have developed over 50 value‑added products from cashew apple, many remain on the shelves’

title: Two workers killed, one injured in compressor blast at Ghaziabad factory
content: "Tribhuvan was taken to Max Hospital, while Imran and Banke were admitted to Safdarjung Hospital. Imran died during treatment at Safdarjung Hospital, while Tribhuvan succumbed at Max Hospital. Banke is undergoing treatment," says Mr. Saxena

title: Bengaluru civic polls: Parties divided over timing
content: While former councillors say that all preliminary groundwork has been completed, they remain divided over the SEC’s decision, with some calling the move “hurried”.

title: RBI officers seek Governor's intervention to correct promotion policy
content: RBI officers across various regional offices and the central bank's headquarters in Mumbai staged protests on May 8 against recent policy changes that link promotions to vacancy availability

title: PM Modi, leaders pays homage to Tagore on Rabindra Jayanti
content: Tagore's birth anniversary is celebrated on 'Pochishe Boishakh' or the 25th of the Bengali month of Boishak, known as Rabindra Jayanti

title: EC publishes Index Cards for Assembly polls
content: According to the data, total votes polled in West Bengal were 6,38,42,843, of which 6,32,58,138 were valid votes. In Assam, the corresponding figures were 2,16,84,656 and 2,13,97,936. Tamil Nadu polled a total of 4,93,89,958 votes and 4,91,24,329 were valid while in Kerala, 2,16,30,208 votes were polled and 2,14,71,988 were valid

title: Casual sexism and ‘harmless’ jokes force women into silence at workplaces, says panel
content: Speakers at Telangana’s ‘Stand With Her’ discussion say microaggression, tokenism and gender stereotypes continue to shape emotionally unsafe environments for women at work and home

title: New invasive moth threatens Ladakh’s farm-based economy
content: The larvae of the Phalera cf. bucephala, commonly called the buff-tip moth, are voracious feeders, capable of stripping an entire tree bare in a few weeks

title: Suvendu Adhikari takes oath as first BJP CM of West Bengal; five others sworn in as Ministers
content: Governor R.N. Ravi administered the oath of office and secrecy to Suvendu Adhikari and five others in the presence of PM Narendra Modi, Union Ministers, Chief Ministers of NDA-ruled States and senior BJP leaders

title: West Bengal oath-taking updates: Suvendu Adhikari sworn in as first BJP CM of West Bengal, five other MLAs also take oath
content: Dilip Ghosh, Agnimitra Paul, Ashok Kirtania, Kshudiram Tudu, and Nisith Pramanik took oath of office as Ministers

title: Tamil Nadu government formation: IUML denies reports of support to Vijay’s TVK
content: “We have been part of the DMK alliance for several decades and there is no question of leaving it. If Mr. Vijay needs support, he should approach DMK president M.K. Stalin, since he, too, has urged the Governor to take steps to expedite formation of a new government,” Kader Mohideen told The Hindu.

title: Supreme Court asks West Bengal Governor to appoint VCs to three state-aided universities
content: A dispute was going on between the governor and Banerjee over the appointment of VCs in 36 state-aided universities

title: Hyderabad’s Gachibowli IT corridor chokes under 15-lakh-tonne waste mountain
content: Nearly 15 lakh tonnes of construction and demolition waste may have accumulated in the area over the years, so much that even under ideal conditions, clearing it could take more than a year

title: GBA elections in Bengaluru to be held between June 14 and 24: State Election Commissioner
content: Inputs were also taken from the commissioners of the five corporations, who serve as ex-officio members, before moving ahead with the scheduling exercise.

title: Jewar MLA expresses concern over proposed high UDF and passenger charges at Noida International Airport, seeks reassessment
content: Dhirendra Singh has sent a detailed representation to the Prime Minister, Uttar Pradesh Chief Minister, Union Civil Aviation Minister and Chief Secretary of the Government of Uttar Pradesh seeking reassessment of charges

title: POCSO case booked against Minister Bandi Sanjay’s son after minor’s complaint
content: Police said the complainant, a Class 10 dropout, alleged that the incident took place during a visit to the farmhouse and that a case was registered after her family approached the police

title: Government appoints Lt Gen NS Raja Subramani (Retd) as next Chief of Defence Staff
content: Lieutenant General NS Raja Subramani is currently Military Adviser, National Security Council Secretariat from September 1, 2025

title: Government appoints Vice Admiral Krishna Swaminathan as next Chief of Naval Staff
content: The present Chief of the Naval Staff Admiral Dinesh Kumar Tripathi, PVSM, AVSM, NM will retire from service on May 31, 2026

title: VCK to support TVK, announcement soon
content: Sources said VCK Legislature Party leader Vanniarasu is on his way to Chennai to sign the letter of support

title: India, IOM discuss expanding, diversifying regular migration pathways
content: "Discussions focussed on expanding and diversifying regular migration pathways and on India’s proactive approach to the implementation of GCM and IOM activities in India," Kirti Vardhan Singh said

title: Suvendu Adhikari: All you need to know about West Bengal’s first BJP CM
content: Known for his leadership in protests and opposition to the previous government, Suvendu Adhikari’s journey from the Trinamool Congress to the BJP has reshaped the political landscape of Bengal

title: Kochi Corporation plans cloud kitchen for Indira Canteens
content: Indira Canteen, which is nearing two months of operation, has reportedly proved viable through cross-subsidy.

title: Same family, different politics
content: From the Gandhis on a national level to the KCR family in Telangana and the Chautalas in Haryana, political families across states have been split on party lines

title: ED raids Punjab Minister Sanjeev Arora, linked persons again
content: The action was taken under the criminal sections of the Prevention of Money Laundering Act (PMLA), the officials said

title: ‘Want to live in India? sing Vande Mataram’, says Haryana Minister Anil Vij
content: “Vande Mataram is our national song and holds the same importance as the national anthem. Singing and respecting it is just as essential as respecting Jana Gana Mana If one wants to live in India, one must sing Vande Mataram,” Mr. Vij said

title: Nitish and BJP have no moral right to speak on dynastic politics, says Tejashwi Yadav
content: Tejashwi Yadav pointed out that without contesting any elections or holding membership in any house, Nitish Kumar’s son, Nishant, and Upendra Kushwaha’s son, Deepak Prakash, have been appointed Ministers

title: Only Congress party can defeat BJP in the battle of ideologies, says Rahul Gandhi
content: Rahul Gandhi joins Haryana Congress leader’s ‘Sadbhav Yatra’, accuses BJP of attacking democratic system and Ambedkar’s Constitution

title: Wet spell and easterlies likely to bring relief from peak summer heat in Tamil Nadu for a week
content: According to the RMC, many places over South T.N. and a few places in the northern districts are likely to receive light to moderate rain over the weekend; prevailing weather systems will also bring light to moderate rainfall in some areas of Chennai on Saturday and reduce day temperature

title: Bihar Government offers ‘One Time Traffic Challan Settlement Scheme’ for violators
content: They will get 50% relaxation in depositing the challan fee. Facility can be availed at the Lok Adalat held in every district from May 09. Road accident victims, under the PM RAHAT scheme, can avail cashless treatment of up to ₹1.5 lakh

title: ‘Amma Ariyan’: Restored version of Kerala filmmaker John Abraham’s cult classic to premiere at Cannes
content: The film with a cult following, restored by the not-for-profit organisation Film Heritage Foundation, is the only Indian feature film selected for world premiere at the festival this year. The premiere is scheduled on May 16

title: Cricket gives special dimension to India-Trinidad and Tobago ties: Jaishankar
content: External Affairs Minister S. Jaishankar arrived in Port of Spain from Paramaribo on May 8, 2026 on the concluding leg of his three-nation tour of Jamaica, Suriname, and Trinidad and Tobago

title: Health activists demand stringent action on death of two women in Kota
content: While two women died in the incident, several others have developed serious health complications, including kidney failure

title: Karnataka High Court permits two transgender persons to continue hormone replacement therapy
content: The petitioners complain that the doctors at the private hospital, where they have been undergoing treatment, have declined to continue it due to a recent amendment made to the Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Amendment Act, 2026.

title: Kerala Assembly Polls 2026: Narrow victory triggers political blame game in Kottarakara
content: Congress MP alleges a high-stakes tactical deal between CPI(M) and BJP led to Balagopal’s victory; UDF faces internal turmoil over losing votes in its own bastion of Ezhukone

title: Father alleges foul play in daughter’s death at Krosuru police station in Palnadu district
content: The woman had filed a complaint in 2021 against a police constable. The Palnadu SP says she fell from the first floor of the building during the inquiry

title: Educational institutions must ensure bus safety compliance by May 20, warn officials
content: Inspections of buses operated by educational institutions began on May 1 and will cover 1,571 buses across the NTR district

title: Census 2027: Sindhudurg village becomes first in Maharashtra to complete self-enumeration
content: The village in Vengurla taluka, which comprises 36 houses and a population of 144, achieved the milestone recently under the guidance of Collector Tripti Dhodmise, officials said

title: Stop demolitions, sealing houses under garb of anti-drug campaign in Kashmir: J&K Minister Sakina Itoo
content: The Minister also expressed concern over “more demolitions and attachment of properties in the Kashmir valley compared to Jammu”

title: Two arrested With MDMA and ganja at Vizhinjam in Thiruvananthapuram
content: They are suspected to have targeted tourists who visit popular destinations such as Kovalam and Poovar to peddle the contraband

title: ‘Extremely shy’ to direct arrest as first option, no need to sensationalise probe: SC in Anil Ambani’s ADAG ‘bank fraud’ case
content: CBI report estimates loss to public sector banks and LIC at over ₹27,000 crore in seven cases involving Anil Ambani’s ADAG and its firms. Top court says arrest must be last resort, and not first option.

title: Kerala polls: LDF will overcome setback and make strong comeback, says CPI(M) leader E.P. Jayarajan
content: In a Facebook post, Jayarajan says the scale of the defeat suffered by Left Democratic Front in Assembly election was “completely unexpected”

title: Watermelon yields good turnover for Kudumbashree women farmers
content: Kudumbashree’s women farmers in Kerala have reported a remarkable turnover of ₹6.78 crore from watermelon cultivation on 758 acres as part of the ‘Venal Madhuram’ initiative this year, nearly doubling last year’s earnings.

title: Has Mamata Banerjee Finally Conceded? Updated X Bio Drops A Big Hint
content: Mamata Banerjee updated her X bio this morning to reflect that she was the chief minister in three previous terms.

title: Opinion: Opinion | No Permanent Rivals In Politics As Vijay Turns Tamil Nadu Into 3-Way Battle
content: Back in 2006, when former Chief Minister J Jayalalithaa formed an alliance with MDMK chief Vaiko, she told waiting reporters that "there are no permanent friends or allies in politics."

title: Probe Agency Raids Punjab Minister's Premises, Bhagwant Mann Hits Out At BJP
content: The ED on Saturday carried out searches at four locations connected to Arora and his entities under the Prevention of Money Laundering Act (PMLA), 2002

title: Tamil Nadu Government Formation LIVE: VCK Keeps Tamil Nadu On Edge, Says Decision To Back Vijay At 4 PM
content: Tamil Nadu Election Results 2026 Live Updates: There is buzz that the Dravidian arch-rivals DMK and AIADMK may come together after over 50 years to form a government and keep Vijay out of power.

title: VCK Keeps Vijay Guessing, Decision On Support To Be Announced This Evening
content: Last minute hiccups followed what appeared as a done deal last evening, with Vijay currently having just 116 MLA-elects on his side.

title: "AI-Generated": AMMK's TTV Dhinakaran Trashes Letter Of Support To Vijay
content: The TVK had reached out to the AMMK for the support of its lone MLA-elect Kamaraj S to stitch together a working coalition.

title: Video: PM Bows Down With Folded Hands To Public At Suvendu Adhikari's Oath
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi had spent considerable time campaigning in West Bengal ahead of the assembly election

title: Probe Agency Raids Premises Linked To Punjab Minister Sanjeev Arora, 2nd In A Month
content: Chandigarh, May 9 (IANS) The Enforcement Directorate (ED) on Saturday conducted searches at four premises linked to Punjab Industry Minister Sanjeev Arora and his associates under the Prevention of Money Laundering Act (PMLA) of 2002.

title: PM Modi To Visit Hyderabad Tomorrow, First Trip Since 2024 Poll Victory
content: During the visit, PM Modi will inaugurate and lay foundation stones for development projects collectively valued at around Rs 7,823 crore.

title: North Korea Adopts Policy For Nuclear Strike If Kim Jon Un Is Killed: Report
content: The move comes months after top Iranian leaders, including Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and several senior advisers, were assassinated by US and Israeli military operations.

title: Bright Lights, Hot Orbs: New UFO Files Detail Sightings, Leave Questions Unanswered
content: The documents include more than 20 video files showing unidentified objects captured by military sensors in locations from Syria and Japan to North America

title: Trump Focuses On Reopening Hormuz, Puts Iran Nuclear Talks On Hold
content: Supporters of Trump's war point to Iran's decimated navy and air force as evidence the conflict has weakened Washington's geopolitical foe.

title: Dilip Ghosh, Agnimitra Paul Among 5 In Suvendu Adhikari's Cabinet
content: The five ministers took the oath in the presence of Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Union Home Minister Amit Shah, among other top dignitaries.

title: Mexico Announces Early End To School Year Citing World Cup Preparations
content: Education Secretary Mario Delgado said the decision to end the school year 40 days early was made in part due to a heat wave impacting several states.

title: Suvendu Adhikari Takes Oath As BJP's First Bengal Chief Minister
content: It's quite likely that in the current context no other leader in West Bengal has a more dramatic, almost stardom-like ascension than Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari's story

title: PM Modi Touches Feet Of 97-Year-Old Makhan Lal Sarkar In Bengal: Who Is He?
content: Makhan Lal Sarkar was the aide of Shyama Prasad Mookerjee and part of his last journey, said Bengal BJP chief Samik Bhattacharya.

title: S Gurumurthy Flags Rookie Mistake By Vijay's TVK That Led To Tamil Nadu High Drama
content: S Gurumuthy told NDTV that MK Stalin's DMK is not in a position to give up direct power and is seeking to have indirect power through AIADMK.

title: 'First Get Support, Then Approach Governor': MK Stalin's DMK Slams Vijay
content: Vijay, who approached the Governor with a coalition government, is falling short of the majority mark of 118.

title: From Dilip Ghosh to Agnimitra Paul - what BJP’s first West Bengal cabinet looks like
content: Suvendu Adhikari took oath as the new Chief Minister of West Bengal. The BJP secured a significant win in the 2026 assembly elections. Several prominent leaders attended the swearing-in ceremony. The new government includes ministers like Dilip Ghosh, Agnimitra Paul, Ashok Kirtania, Kshudiram Tudu, and Nisith Pramanik. The state saw record voter turnout.

title: Watch: PM Modi bows down with folded hands before Kolkata crowd as BJP forms first govt in Bengal
content: Prime Minister Modi expressed profound gratitude to the people of Bengal, bowing with folded hands after the BJP's historic Assembly election victory. Alongside new chief minister Adhikari, he shared an emotional moment with 98-year-old veteran Makhanlal Sarkar, even touching his feet in a gesture of deep respect. This marks a significant shift in West Bengal's political landscape.

title: Indian sailor killed after dhow catches fire near Strait of Hormuz; 17 rescued
content: A fire aboard a wooden dhow near the Strait of Hormuz resulted in the death of one Indian sailor and injuries to four others. Seventeen Indian crew members were rescued by a passing vessel. The incident occurred amidst heightened regional tensions, with recent exchanges between Iran and the United States in the vital shipping lane.

title: Krunal reacts to viral Pooran collar-grabbing incident: 'No lafda, only ...'
content: Krunal Pandya dismissed rumours of tension with Nicholas Pooran after a viral IPL 2026 video showed Pooran grabbing him by the collar during a chat. Krunal later posted, “No lafda, only love,” while Pooran replied, “Only love here.” The exchange followed an on-field spat during the Lucknow Super Giants vs Royal Challengers Bengaluru clash.

title: Supreme Court in no mood to condone Madhya Pradesh minister in Col Sofiya Qureshi case
content: The Supreme Court expressed strong disapproval of Madhya Pradesh minister Kunwar Vijay Shah's controversial remarks, questioning the state government's decision on prosecuting him. Despite the Solicitor General suggesting the minister intended praise, the bench noted a lack of repentance and the statement's unfortunate impact. The court also directed the SIT to investigate other instances of the minister's objectionable statements.

title: After 43 yrs, US family sells electrical firm for $1.7bn; gives 540 workers $240m
content: A US-based businessman, Graham Walker, gifted his 540 employees a staggering $240 million from the $1.7 billion sale of his family's company, Fibrebond Corp. This generous act, where workers received an average of $443,000, stemmed from a condition Walker imposed before the sale to power-management giant Eaton.

title: 'Abhi kya karu mai phir?': Rohit frustrated by female fan's selfie act - Watch
content: Rohit Sharma went viral after an awkward selfie moment with a female fan outside the Mumbai Indians bus. As the fan realised her phone had switched off, Rohit jokingly replied, “Toh abhi kya karu mai phir?” The clip sparked laughs online. Meanwhile, Harbhajan Singh praised Rohit’s recent impact and said his earlier return from injury could have changed Mumbai’s IPL season.

title: Caretaker held for ‘rape’ of 3-year-old in Delhi school gets bail
content: A 57-year-old school caretaker, arrested for alleged sexual assault of a three-year-old, has been granted bail by a Dwarka court. The court noted that CCTV footage placed the accused away from the crime scene, and the child's medical examination revealed no injuries. Police had opposed bail, citing the seriousness of the Pocso Act charges.

title: Watch: PM Modi touches feet of 98-year old Makhanlal Sarkar - who is he?
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi honored 98-year-old BJP veteran Makhanlal Sarkar at the party's first West Bengal government swearing-in. Sarkar, a key grassroots leader since the BJP's inception, was recognized for his decades of service and association with the nationalist movement. His arrest in Kashmir in 1952 while with Syama Prasad Mukherjee highlights his early commitment.

title: 'You have 10 working days to voluntarily recognise unions': Google UK staff tells company
content: Google's DeepMind employees are demanding union recognition amid concerns over their AI technology's use by the US and Israeli militaries. Workers seek a commitment against developing AI weapons and surveillance tools, urging management to voluntarily recognize their unions or face legal action. The move highlights growing anxieties about AI's ethical implications and its potential for harm.

title: Jaiswal, Shafali receive NADA notices for missed dope tests
content: Yashasvi Jaiswal and Shafali Verma received notices from India’s anti-doping agency NADA after missing scheduled dope tests, leading to their first “whereabouts failure.” The Board of Control for Cricket in India confirmed the development and said it would ensure stricter compliance, especially with cricket returning to the Olympics in 2028.

title: Sridhar Vembu responds to Meta engineer’s post saying, 'layoffs will continue till we learn to use AI'
content: Zoho founder Sridhar Vembu echoed Meta engineer Arnav Gupta's views on AI-driven job cuts. Both highlight that escalating AI costs, coupled with rising server prices, are forcing tech companies to reduce headcount. This economic response aims to control expenses, rather than solely reflecting an 'AI productivity miracle' at present.  Layoffs continue until companies effectively leverage AI for tangible outcomes.

title: ‘All that will be left of you is what was in your heart': Quote of the day by Jim Carrey
content: Jim Carrey, in a 2014 commencement address, shared profound wisdom, stating that material gains are fleeting and will eventually decay. He emphasized that true value lies in the kindness and intentions held within one's heart, as these are the only things that endure beyond worldly possessions and fame.

title: Foreign investor share falls to 14-year low; DII ownership rises to steady markets
content: Foreign investors are significantly reducing their stake in Indian equities, with FII ownership hitting a multi-year low. Concurrently, domestic institutional investors, particularly mutual funds fueled by SIPs, are increasing their holdings, absorbing much of the foreign selling and reshaping market ownership.

title: BJP’s ‘sonar’ era begins in West Bengal: Suvendu Adhikari takes oath as CM– top developments
content: West Bengal ushers in a new political era as Suvendu Adhikari takes oath as the state's first BJP Chief Minister. This historic win, following an overwhelming mandate, marks the saffron party's long-fought ideological battle. Adhikari's dramatic rise, from local councillor to defeating Mamata Banerjee, culminates in this significant power shift.

title: 'This is not my first IPL': Miller frustrated after being benched by DC
content: David Miller expressed frustration at being benched for Delhi Capitals' recent IPL 2026 matches, acknowledging the team's focus on overseas player combinations. Despite missing out, the veteran batter remains committed to supporting the squad and staying prepared for any future opportunities.

title: Piyush Goyal meets LVHM, L’Oreal to push India manufacturing and exports
content: Union Minister Piyush Goyal met with luxury giants LVMH and L'Oreal to boost India's manufacturing, exports, and investments. Discussions centered on expanding production, nurturing local talent, and integrating Indian culture into global luxury supply chains. The talks also explored new avenues for India's beauty and personal care sector, leveraging L'Oreal's recent tech hub launch in Hyderabad.

title: BCCI cracks down on IPL ‘reel culture’; players and families warned
content: Commentators Reprimanded, Players & Families Warned As BCCI, Franchises Begin Crackdown On Social-Media Practices That Invite Potential Anti-Corruption Breaches

title: Two Indians in crew of hantavirus-hit ship
content: A rare hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship with two Indian crew members has sparked international attention. Experts assure no immediate concern for India, citing previous sporadic cases. The Andes strain, linked to limited human-to-human spread, requires close exposure, unlike COVID-19. India's healthcare is prepared for potential cases.

title: Trump threatens 'Project Freedom plus' if Iran deal not reached — what it means
content: US President Donald Trump indicated a potential revival of 'Project Freedom' in the Strait of Hormuz if a deal with Iran falters. This initiative, aimed at ensuring ship movement, was temporarily paused following requests from Pakistan and other nations, citing military successes and progress towards an agreement. The blockade of Iran's ports, however, will continue.

title: ED raids Punjab minister Sanjeev Arora's home in money laundering case
content: The Enforcement Directorate (ED) conducted fresh searches on Saturday against Punjab Industries Minister Sanjeev Arora and associates as part of a money laundering probe. Raids targeted Arora's official residence in Chandigarh and a Gurugram real estate firm. This follows previous actions under FEMA and a 2024 money laundering investigation.

title: 'He was on the stairs': Iran claims Mojtaba was knocked down by US blast but is 'recovering well'
content: Iran's Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei is reportedly in good health, with officials dismissing rumors of severe injuries following recent strikes. While acknowledging minor wounds, they insist he remains active in state affairs. Meanwhile, the US awaits Iran's response to a proposal aimed at ending the West Asia conflict, with officials expressing hope for a serious offer.

title: More hunting, few limits: Trump lifts restrictions in national parks and wilderness areas
content: The Trump administration is expanding hunting and fishing access on federal lands, including national parks and wildlife refuges, by removing existing restrictions. Interior Secretary Doug Burgum stated the policy is to open lands unless specific exceptions apply. This move aims to boost conservation and rural economies, despite concerns about visitor safety and science-based management.

title: Trump announces 3-day Russia-Ukraine ceasefire, 1,000-prisoner swap
content: US President Trump announced a three-day Russia-Ukraine ceasefire from May 9-11, hoping for a long-term peace deal and a prisoner exchange. Despite this, fighting persisted, with both sides reporting attacks. Ukraine accused Russia of continuing assaults, while Russia claimed to intercept numerous drones. The truce follows Russia's Victory Day, a proposal Kyiv had previously dismissed.

title: Voter support or MLAs' backing? Congress weighs its options in Kerala
content: Congress leadership is deliberating on the Kerala CM post, weighing intra-party support for KC Venugopal against public sentiment favoring VD Satheesan. A decision is expected by Sunday, with discussions involving key state leaders and central party chiefs in Delhi.

title: Sanjiv Goenka doesn't interrupt Virat Kohli, wins heart - Watch
content: Lucknow Super Giants secured a crucial win against Royal Challengers Bengaluru. LSG owner Sanjiv Goenka showed respect by waiting to meet Virat Kohli. He patiently waited with his grandson, not interrupting Kohli's conversation. Later, Kohli warmly interacted with them and signed an autograph for the young fan. This moment went viral online.

title: Kolkata Police unfollows Mamata on X; follows PM Modi, Amit Shah after BJP win
content: Kolkata Police's official X handle has shifted its following to prominent BJP leaders like PM Modi and Amit Shah, unfollowing Mamata Banerjee and Abhishek Banerjee. This digital change follows the BJP's assembly election victory and coincides with the withdrawal of additional security from TMC offices and residences.

title: Neeraj Chopra nears comeback after 232 days: What is next for him?
content: Neeraj Chopra is nearing a comeback after a 232-day break focused on rehabilitation from groin, ankle and back injuries. The Olympic champion has been rebuilding fitness and refining technique ahead of the 2028 Los Angeles Olympics. Potential return events include meets in Poland, Finland and Czechia, while his main target remains defending his Asian Games title later this year.

title: No tap, no toilet, no power: The cracks within ST households
content: Telangana's development narrative is challenged by a stark reality: thousands lack basic amenities like toilets, tap water, and electricity. A recent survey reveals deep inequalities, particularly affecting Scheduled Tribes and Castes, highlighting persistent social and economic backwardness despite claims of progress. This disparity impacts health, dignity, and overall quality of life for many.

title: Blood-stained cloth stuffed in mouth: New details in retd IPS officer’s wife’s murder
content: A chilling case has emerged in Hyderabad, where Tanuja Ranjan, the spouse of a former IPS officer, was found dead in her home. Investigators suspect foul play by a domestic worker and an accomplice, who allegedly smothered her after restraining her. It appears that the motive behind this horrific act was theft, with significant valuables disappearing from the scene.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' BO day 51: Ranveer Singh film eyes Rs 1800 cr amid buzz around third installment
content: Ranveer Singh's 'Dhurandhar 2' is nearing a massive Rs 1800 crore worldwide milestone in its 8th week.  Meanwhile, makers have hinted at 'Dhurandhar 3,' suggesting the spy saga is far from over, potentially surprising audiences later this year with new developments.

title: China'a trade figures: Exports jump 14.1% in April, ahead of Trump-Xi visit
content: China's exports surged 14.1% in April, significantly exceeding expectations and bolstering the nation's economy despite global uncertainties. This strong performance precedes a crucial meeting between US and Chinese leaders, where trade tensions and other disputes are expected to be discussed. Analysts anticipate continued export-driven growth for China this year.

title: Happy mother's day: Top 75 wishes, messages, images, greetings and quotes to share
content: This Mother's Day, move beyond generic greetings to honor the women who raised you with meaningful messages. The article offers categorized heartfelt, witty, and minimalist quotes, acknowledging the "mental load" and celebrating "mothers of choice."

title: Tess Daly and Vernon Kay split after 22 years of marriage
content: Popular presenters Tess Daly and Vernon Kay have announced their amicable separation after 22 years of marriage. The couple, who share two daughters, stated the decision was made with mutual respect and understanding, emphasizing their continued commitment to co-parenting. They have requested privacy during this transition.

title: Busted! Virat Kohli's RCB match ticket turns out to be AI-generated
content: Lucknow Super Giants secured their maiden home win in IPL 2026 against Royal Challengers Bengaluru, but the match was marred by the discovery of fake AI-generated tickets. Police apprehended four individuals from Chhattisgarh for using AI tools and graphic software to create and sell counterfeit tickets outside the Ekana Stadium.

title: After Gukesh's viral 'first pump' celebration, Sindarov gets revenge - Watch
content: Javokhir Sindarov defeated D Gukesh in their first-ever blitz clash at the Grand Chess Tour in Warsaw, avenging his earlier rapid loss to the World Champion. Gukesh’s rare fist-pump celebration after that previous win had gone viral. With one win each, their growing rivalry now heads into a decisive final reverse-blitz showdown before this year’s World Championship clash.
